description: Bash scripting constructs, builtins, and patterns for writing robust shell scripts

categories.name: Script Structure
categories.description: Boilerplate, headers, and foundational patterns every script should have

commands.name: shebang & set flags
commands.summary: Define the interpreter and enable strict mode to catch errors early

options.description: Exit immediately if any command returns non-zero
Catch failures inside pipelines (not just the last command)
Print each command before executing it (debug trace)
ERR traps are inherited by functions and subshells

examples.description: Standard strict-mode header for all scripts
Enable debug tracing for a block then disable it

commands.note: set -u will cause issues if you use $@ or $* on an empty argument list. Use ${1:-} as a safe default.

commands.name: trap
commands.summary: Run cleanup code when the script exits, errors, or receives a signal

options.description: Runs on any exit (normal or error) — best for cleanup
Runs when a command fails (combine with set -E)
Reset a trap to its default behavior

examples.description: Clean up a temp directory on exit
Print error location on failure
Graceful shutdown on SIGTERM
examples.code: trap 'echo "Shutting down..."; cleanup; exit 0' TERM INT

commands.name: Functions
commands.summary: Define reusable blocks of code with local variables and return values

options.description: Declare a variable scoped to the function only
Exit function with a status code (0 = success)
Positional arguments passed to the function
Declare a constant that cannot be reassigned

examples.description: Function with local variables and return code
Logging helper with timestamp
Return a value via stdout (command substitution)

categories.name: Variables & Parameters
categories.description: Variable assignment, expansion, defaults, and string manipulation

commands.name: Parameter Expansion
commands.summary: Manipulate variables inline — defaults, substrings, replacements, lengths

options.description: Use default if VAR is unset or empty
Assign and use default if VAR is unset or empty
Error and exit if VAR is unset or empty
Use value only if VAR is set (else empty)
Length of the string in VAR
Substring: start at offset, take len characters
Replace first occurrence of old with new
Replace all occurrences of old with new
Strip shortest match of pattern from the front
Strip longest match of pattern from the front
Strip shortest match of pattern from the end
Strip longest match of pattern from the end

examples.description: Use a default value if argument not provided
Require a variable to be set or fail
Strip path from filename (like basename)
Strip extension from filename
Replace all spaces with underscores

commands.name: Special Variables
commands.summary: Built-in variables that Bash automatically populates

options.description: All arguments as separate quoted strings
All arguments as a single word
Exit code of the last command
PID of the last background process
Current line number in the script
Path of the script being executed
Array of exit codes from last pipeline

examples.description: Check if a required argument was passed
Get the directory where the script lives
Capture exit code of a command

commands.name: Arrays
commands.summary: Indexed and associative arrays for storing lists and key-value pairs

options.description: All keys of an associative array

examples.description: Loop over all elements of an array
examples.code:
services=(api worker scheduler)
for svc in "${services[@]}"; do
  echo "Starting $svc"
done

examples.description: Associative array as a lookup table
examples.code:
declare -A PORTS=([api]=8080 [db]=5432 [cache]=6379)
echo "API runs on port ${PORTS[api]}"

examples.description: Capture command output into an array
examples.code:
mapfile -t pods < <(kubectl get pods -o name)
echo "Found ${#pods[@]} pods"

categories.name: Control Flow
categories.description: Conditionals, loops, and flow control constructs

commands.name: if / [[ ]]
commands.summary: Conditional branching with the modern [[ ]] test syntax

options.description: True if FILE exists and is a regular file
True if DIR exists and is a directory
True if FILE exists and is non-empty

examples.description: Check if a required config file exists
examples.code:
if [[ ! -f config.yaml ]]; then
  echo 'ERROR: config.yaml not found' >&2
  exit 1
fi

examples.description: Match a string with regex
Check string is non-empty
examples.code:
if [[ -n "$API_KEY" ]]; then
  echo 'API key is set'
fi

commands.name: for loop
commands.summary: Iterate over lists, ranges, arrays, or command output

examples.description: Loop over environment names
examples.code:
for env in dev staging production; do
  echo "Deploying to $env"
done

examples.description: C-style loop with counter
examples.code:
for (( i=1; i<=5; i++ )); do
  echo "Attempt $i"
done

examples.description: Loop over files matching a glob
examples.code:
for f in /etc/nginx/conf.d/*.conf; do
  nginx -t -c "$f" && echo "$f OK"
done

commands.name: while / until loop
commands.summary: Loop based on a condition or read input line by line

options.description: Read a file or stream line by line
Exit loop or skip to next iteration

examples.description: Process a file line by line
examples.code:
while IFS= read -r line; do
  echo "Processing: $line"
done < input.txt

examples.description: Retry a command up to 5 times with backoff
examples.code:
for i in {1..5}; do
  curl -sf https://api.example.com/health && break
  echo "Retry $i failed, waiting..."
  sleep $((i * 2))
done

examples.description: Wait until a service is ready
examples.code:
until curl -sf http://localhost:8080/health; do
  echo 'Waiting for service...'
  sleep 2
done

commands.name: case statement
commands.summary: Multi-branch matching on string patterns — cleaner than many if/elif chains

options.description: Match a literal or glob pattern
Match either pattern p1 or p2

examples.description: Dispatch based on CLI argument
Match OS type

categories.name: Input & Output
categories.description: Read user input, format output, redirect streams, and handle pipes

commands.name: read
commands.summary: Read input from stdin or a file into variables

options.description: Raw mode: don't interpret backslash escapes
Display a prompt string before reading
Silent mode: don't echo input (for passwords)
Read until DELIM character instead of newline
Set field separator for splitting (empty = preserve whitespace)

examples.description: Prompt for confirmation
Read a password without echoing it
Split a CSV line into an array

commands.name: printf
commands.summary: Formatted output — more reliable than echo for scripting

examples.description: Print a formatted table row
Zero-padded counter
Write to stderr

commands.name: Redirection
commands.summary: Control where stdin, stdout, and stderr flow

options.description: Redirect stderr to wherever stdout goes
Redirect both stdout and stderr to FILE

examples.description: Silence all output from a command
Log stdout to file and also show on terminal
Heredoc to write a multi-line file
Feed a string to a command without a pipe

commands.name: xargs
commands.summary: Build and execute commands from standard input

options.description: Pass at most N arguments per command invocation
Run up to N processes in parallel
Input items are null-delimited (use with find -print0)
Don't run command if input is empty

examples.description: Delete files found by find (null-safe)
Run a command on each item in parallel
Pass multiple lines as individual args

categories.name: Text Processing
categories.description: Transform, filter, and extract data from text streams and files

commands.name: cut
commands.summary: Extract specific columns or character ranges from each line

options.description: Use DELIM as the field separator

examples.description: Extract the username from /etc/passwd
Get the 2nd and 3rd CSV columns

commands.name: sort
commands.summary: Sort lines of text

examples.description: Sort a CSV by 3rd column numerically
Sort by size (from du output)

commands.name: uniq
commands.summary: Report or remove adjacent duplicate lines (always sort first)

options.description: Prefix each line with its occurrence count

examples.description: Count and rank most common log errors
examples.code: grep 'ERROR' app.log | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -10

commands.name: tr
commands.summary: Translate or delete characters in a stream

examples.description: Convert string to lowercase
Remove all newlines from output
Replace colons with newlines for readability

commands.name: jq
commands.summary: Parse, filter, and transform JSON from the command line

options.description: Iterate over all elements of array or object
Extract a field from each array element
Pass a shell variable as a jq variable
Length of array, string, or object

examples.description: Extract all pod names from kubectl JSON output
Filter array by a field value
Construct a new JSON object from fields
Pass a shell variable into jq

categories.name: Error Handling
categories.description: Patterns for writing scripts that fail safely and report clearly

commands.name: exit codes
commands.summary: Return meaningful exit codes and handle errors from commands

options.description: Exit code of the last command
Allow a command to fail silently
Run CMD only if previous succeeded

examples.description: Exit with custom error message function
examples.code:
die() { printf 'ERROR: %s\n' "$*" >&2; exit 1; }

[[ -f config.yaml ]] || die 'config.yaml not found'

examples.description: Run a command and capture exit code
examples.code:
if ! my_command; then
  echo 'Command failed' >&2
  exit 1
fi

examples.description: Allow a non-critical command to fail

commands.name: getopts
commands.summary: Parse short command-line flags and arguments in a script

options.description: Index of the next argument to process
Shift past all parsed options to reach positional args

examples.description: Script with -e (environment) and -v (verbose) flags

commands.name: mktemp
commands.summary: Create unique temporary files or directories safely

options.description: Create a temporary directory instead of a file
Use PREFIX for the temp name (deprecated; use template)
Template: X's are replaced with random characters

examples.description: Create a temp dir and auto-clean it on exit
Create a temp file for intermediate output

commands.name: flock
commands.summary: Prevent concurrent script executions using file-based locking

options.description: Non-blocking: fail immediately if lock can't be acquired
File to use as the lock target

examples.description: Ensure only one instance of a script runs at a time
Wrap an entire script with a lock

categories.name: Process & Job Control
categories.description: Run commands in background, parallel, and manage subshells

commands.name: Background & wait
commands.summary: Run commands asynchronously and wait for them to complete

options.description: PID of the last backgrounded process
Wait for a specific PID to finish
Wait for all background jobs to finish

examples.description: Run tasks in parallel and wait for all to finish
examples.code:
deploy_service api &
deploy_service worker &
deploy_service scheduler &
wait
echo 'All deployments complete'

examples.description: Parallel with exit code checking
examples.code:
pids=()
for svc in "${services[@]}"; do
  deploy "$svc" & pids+=($!)
done
for pid in "${pids[@]}"; do
  wait "$pid" || echo "PID $pid failed"
done

commands.name: Subshells & Command Substitution
commands.summary: Capture command output and isolate variable scope

options.description: Command substitution: capture stdout as a string
Subshell: run CMD in an isolated environment
Process substitution: treat CMD output as a file
Process substitution: write into CMD's stdin

examples.description: Capture multi-line output into a variable
Compare two command outputs as files
Run a block without affecting parent shell vars
examples.code:
(cd /tmp && tar xzf archive.tar.gz)
# Original directory is unchanged
